experiments.hypothesis: LLM soft ranking remains blocked by missing replay archives; recovering already-saved raw llm_output files into llm_prompt_resp files should increase replayable candidate coverage without changing strategy behavior.

LLM soft ranking is blocked because replay attribution cannot yet distinguish all usable ranking-eligible LLM samples from headline dated replay coverage; improving LLM replay attribution coverage makes the ranking alpha test auditable without changing strategy behavior.

LLM soft ranking is blocked because replay attribution cannot yet expose archive metadata from covered LLM response files independently of broad backtester state; adding read-only replay attribution helpers for LLM archives makes usable coverage auditable without touching strategy behavior.

Legacy LLM replay attribution remains partially unauditable when saved response files lack archive_context; a narrow audit artifact can identify which existing replay days can be backfilled from decision logs without changing strategy behavior.

Financials trend winners may be target-truncated by the shared target width; widen only `trend_long | Financials` to 6.0 ATR.
Trend Industrials restoration can be alpha-positive only in an extremely risk-on tape; restore 0.10x risk when min(SPY, QQQ) pct_from_ma is at least 10%, otherwise keep the current 0x rule.

Same-day sleeve ordering may be a cleaner allocation lever than wider scarce-slot deferral: when candidates compete for entry slots, rank trend_long ahead of breakout_long without changing signal generation, exits, sizing, LLM/news replay, or earnings.

`breakout_long | Commodities` may have convex winners that are clipped by the shared target width; wider ATR targets for this sleeve could improve EV without changing entries, sizing, LLM/news, add-ons, scarce-slot routing, or other exits.
A trend-only overflow slot can recover the useful part of wider capacity without repeating global MAX_POSITIONS expansion: use MAX_POSITIONS=6 while deferring breakout_long whenever slots <= 2.

`breakout_long | Technology` may contain convex winners that the shared breakout target clips too early; wider ATR targets for this sleeve could improve EV without changing entries, sizing, LLM/news, add-ons, scarce-slot routing, or other exits.

The accepted day-2 follow-through add-on is directionally correct, but 25% of original shares under-allocates to confirmed winners. Raising only the add-on fraction should improve EV and PnL across the fixed windows while preserving existing caps and triggers.

After promoting the day-2 follow-through add-on fraction to 50%, the add-on-specific position cap may still clip confirmed winners. Raising only ADDON_MAX_POSITION_PCT should improve EV without changing entries, exits, trigger thresholds, LLM/news replay, scarce-slot routing, or the initial-entry single-position cap.
A strict extension weak-followthrough subset may identify post-entry failures without the broader winner collateral observed in exp-20260427-022.
Stable LLM/event replay coverage and attribution are required before LLM/event ranking alpha can be evaluated.

Accepted-stack bad trades may be better explained by overlap among prior reproducible loss families than by another new price-only trigger; a priority taxonomy can quantify dominant families, tail-loss share, and winner collateral for future alpha experiments.
Fresh positive clean-news confirmation may identify higher-quality existing A/B candidates for event-aware ranking without creating a new entry sleeve.
An OHLCV-event-sensitive liquidity-filtered shadow universe can produce replayable, low-overlap candidates across all fixed windows, avoiding the archived-news coverage gap in prior event scouts.
Fresh T1-negative clean-news context may identify lower-quality existing A/B candidates for future event-aware ranking without creating a new entry sleeve.

Accepted-stack bad trades may contain a reproducible post-entry volatility expansion stopout family where realized high-low range expands after entry without enough favorable progress, distinct from prior hold-quality, low-MFE, gap-down, near-target, sector, add-on, extension, and overlap-priority taxonomies.

A sector-relative-strength persistence shadow universe can identify replayable low-overlap candidates whose 10-20 day forward returns justify a future external universe source, without adding tickers to production.

Alpha experiments remain vulnerable to incomparable or incomplete baseline artifacts; a fixed-window baseline reproducibility audit can verify that judge-ready result files contain stable windows and required metrics before the next promotion test.
Fresh negative clean-news context may identify lower-quality existing A/B candidates for future event-aware ranking without creating a new entry sleeve.
A 2% adverse next-open entry cancel improves entry quality by avoiding weak fills that tend to become low-MFE stopouts.
A minimal audit artifact can remove a current measurement blocker for the next alpha experiment without changing strategy behavior.
Accepted-stack bad trades contain a reproducible profit-decay failure family not already explained by recent taxonomies.
A narrow shadow universe can surface replayable low-overlap candidates with useful forward returns without adding production tickers.
Sweeping the upside next-open entry cancel threshold may improve entry execution quality without changing signal generation or exits.
A state-qualified exception to upside next-open cancel for accepted winner-truncation trend sleeves may preserve momentum-confirmed winners without reopening global gap-threshold tuning.

Existing A/B signals that fail to close in the upper part of their own signal-day range may be weaker execution candidates; canceling those next-open entries could improve quality without changing signal generation, exits, sizing, add-ons, or LLM/news replay.
Raising only the initial position cap can improve capital allocation after the accepted 50% add-on showed confirmed winners can absorb more risk budget.
Reduced-risk initial entries may need a lower concentration cap after the accepted 40% initial-cap promotion.
The same-day sector cap may be mis-sized after recent capital-allocation improvements.

After the accepted 40% initial position cap and 50% day-2 add-on, the portfolio heat cap may be the remaining materiality ceiling. A modestly wider heat budget could let confirmed winners receive add-ons without changing entry quality.
When same-day candidates compete for scarce entry slots, globally ranking by existing trade_quality_score may allocate capital to higher-expectancy candidates without changing entries or risk limits.
Context-qualified exceptions to adverse next-open cancel may preserve discounted high-quality entries without reopening the global 2% threshold.
